what_it_does: An acceptance/TDD test script that exercises the NodeRegistry: it discovers node types from the nodes directory, queries the type-graph for producers of 'Text', checks that object_info exposes C2-style fields, runs a small graph through the scheduler using the registry as the node-type source, and finally drops a brand-new node file into a temporary directory to verify the node self-registers, becomes queryable, and executes without any runtime edit.
observations: Uses sys.path.insert to make the parent package importable
Imports NodeInstance, Edge, Graph from contracts.node_record, FsStore from store.fs_store, scheduler from runtime, and NodeRegistry from runtime.registry
Defines NODES_DIR relative to the project root
Maintains a global boolean ok updated by a check(label, cond) helper that prints PASS/FAIL
Asserts the library contains at least constant, uppercase, join, pair, and llm
Calls reg.produces('Text') and checks it includes uppercase, join, and pair
Calls reg.object_info() and checks the llm entry has render_set and ports
Runs a two-node graph via scheduler.run(g, store, reg), passing the registry where node_types previously went
Creates a temporary directory, writes a shout.py node file with VERSION, KIND, PORTS_IN, PORTS_OUT, and a run function
Discovers both NODES_DIR and the temporary directory with a second registry instance
Builds and runs a graph using the new shout node
Verifies the output is 'go!!!' and that no runtime code was edited
standouts: The test encodes the 'drop a new node file and it just works' self-extending property as a concrete assertion
It treats the registry as a drop-in replacement for the old hardcoded node_types dict passed to scheduler.run
It mixes discovery, type-graph querying, object_info schema validation, and end-to-end execution in one script
conventions: Follows a lightweight TDD acceptance-test pattern with a global ok flag and a check helper
Uses tempfile.mkdtemp for both the FsStore working directories and the new-node sandbox
Uses string-based code generation to create the new node file on the fly
concerns: Hardcodes the expected node-type set and object_info shape, so it will fail if the library evolves
Does not clean up the temporary directories it creates
Relies on a global mutable ok flag in check, which is simple but not thread-safe or reusable
Assumes NodeRegistry exposes .types, .produces(...), and .object_info() without documenting their contracts here
notes: This is the acceptance test that defines what the live, self-extending node registry must do before it is implemented.
questions: What exact API does NodeRegistry expose for .types, .produces(type), and .object_info()?
How does discovery map a file like shout.py to the type name 'shout'?
What is the full object_info schema beyond render_set and ports?
What is scheduler.run's expected signature and return shape?
Are node files required to define VERSION, KIND, PORTS_IN, PORTS_OUT, and a run function?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to test a live, discovered, self-extending node-type registry, and the file does exactly that through concrete assertions.
intention_signals: The file is trying to prove that the runtime can learn new node types simply by scanning directories, so adding behavior becomes a file-drop operation rather than a code change.
novelty: It turns the abstract 'self-extending system' goal into a single, runnable acceptance test that writes a new node file mid-execution and asserts it runs without touching the runtime.
contribution: A concrete acceptance test that specifies the registry's expected surface (discovery, type-graph query, object_info, scheduler integration) and the drop-in node extension contract.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test script that verifies NodeRegistry discovers node types from directories, answers type-graph queries, exposes object_info, and allows dropping a new node file to be registered and executed without modifying the runtime.
intention_for_embedding: To enforce the self-extending registry contract through TDD before the registry implementation exists.

symbols.does: Updates a global ok flag by AND-ing it with a boolean condition and prints a PASS/FAIL line for a test label.
symbols.description: A minimal test assertion helper used throughout the script. It mutates the module-level ok variable and emits a human-readable result, making the script self-contained without a full test framework.

symbols.does: Runs the full E4 acceptance test: registry discovery, type-graph queries, object_info validation, scheduler execution via the registry, and a drop-in new-node-file extension test.
symbols.description: The main entry point constructs a NodeRegistry, verifies it discovers the existing node library and can answer producer queries, checks object_info shape, runs a graph through scheduler.run using the registry, then writes a new shout.py node into a temp directory and confirms the new registry instance picks it up and executes it without any runtime modification.
